channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nawaz Sharif criticizes Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari over AJK development during Mirpur rally
item.description: Former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif addressed a rally in Mirpur, criticizing Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari for misrepresenting Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK). He emphasized...

item.title: Federal Support for Fossil Fuels Contradicts Climate Pledges
item.description: The Canadian government's financial backing for the oil and gas sector has drawn criticism from environmental advocates, who argue it undermines a 2023 commitment to phase out fossil fuel subsidies. D...

item.title: Computer model suggests the 2026 FIFA World Cup produced lower emissions than some forecasts despite its larger scale
item.description: The article examines the carbon footprint of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after FIFA did not publish an updated estimate of the tournament's greenhouse gas emissions. Although the original joint bid by the...

item.title: Trial of Matlala continues with key witness testifying in attempted murder case
item.description: The trial of Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala, accused in the attempted murder of actor Tebogo Thobejane and taxi boss Joe Sibanyoni, resumed at Johannesburg's high court. Matlala, his wife Tsakane, and three o...

item.title: Evan Tan's Journey from K-Drama Fan to Content Creator
item.description: Evan Tan, a content creator, shared his journey of transforming his passion for K-dramas into a career that caters to Filipino fans of Korean culture. His story highlights how a deep love for Korean t...

item.title: Zamfara Communities Suffer Deadly Attack As Security Agencies Fail To Respond
item.description: Over 20 residents were killed in a three-hour daylight attack by heavily armed terrorists on Zamfara State communities, with survivors accusing nearby security forces of abandoning their posts. The at...

item.title: 15 Kenyans Among 175 Asylum Seekers After World Cup Visit to Canada
item.description: At least 15 Kenyan nationals are among 175 foreign visitors who submitted refugee claims in Canada after attending the 2026 FIFA World Cup, according to figures released by Immigration, Refugees and C...

item.title: 23 individuals charged with terrorism meeting in Mwanza
item.description: The article reports on a legal case in Mwanza where 23 people were charged with participating in a terrorism-related meeting. The incident occurred on July 24, 2026, and authorities are investigating ...

item.title: Wall Street Navigates Fed Policy and Tech Earnings Amid Market Volatility
item.description: The U.S. stock market faces critical challenges this week as investors await the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and a wave of corporate earnings reports from technology firms and AI leaders ...

item.title: Four men in their 20s die in Merseyside car crash involving lamppost
item.description: Four men in their 20s have died following a fatal car crash in Merseyside, where a black Seat Cupra collided with a lamppost on Liverpool Road, Huyton, just before 2am. The incident occurred while the...

item.title: Trump's 'Freedom Fuel' Policy Sparks Controversy Over Oil Industry Practices
item.description: The article discusses former President Donald Trump's proposed 'Freedom Fuel' initiative, which aims to reshape U.S. energy policies and challenge traditional oil industry practices. The policy has dr...

item.title: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey boosts Melbourne IMAX cinema to record earnings
item.description: Christopher Nolan’s epic film 'The Odyssey' is driving Australia's box office to its highest level in seven years, with Melbourne's IMAX cinema reaping significant benefits. The film has grossed $18 m...

item.title: Astronomers Discover First Stellar-Mass Black Hole in Omega Centauri Cluster
item.description: Using over 20 years of Hubble Space Telescope data and recent James Webb Space Telescope observations, astronomers have identified the first stellar-mass black hole in the Omega Centauri cluster, a ma...

item.title: Mumbai model Rhiya Ahir calls for concrete action after PM Modi's assurance on NEET paper leak reforms
item.description: Model and actor Rhiya Ahir, who became widely recognised after standing in front of a Mumbai Police van during student protests over alleged examination paper leaks, has welcomed Prime Minister Narend...

item.title: NCAA Considers Suspension of Royal Air Maroc Due to Passenger Complaints
item.description: The Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has warned Royal Air Maroc of possible suspension over repeated failures to address passenger complaints. Director Michael Achimugu accused the airline of ...

item.title: Aletta Rose faces psychiatric evaluation after courtroom disruptions
item.description: Aletta Rose, 63, accused of murdering her 79-year-old sister Constance Scholtz in March 2026, has become a focal point in the Bellville Magistrate’s Court due to her repeated disruptive behavior. The ...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran reaffirm commitment to Islamabad MoU amid regional tensions
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar emphasized the importance of fulfilling obligations under the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) during a meeting with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi....

item.title: 23 Individuals Charged with Terror Meeting in Mwanza
item.description: The article discusses the legal proceedings against 23 individuals accused of organizing a terror-related meeting in Mwanza. The charges stem from allegations that the meeting aimed to coordinate acti...

item.title: Ruger Explains Reason Behind His Signature Eye Patch
item.description: Nigerian Afrobeats star Ruger has sparked conversation about his iconic eye patch during a livestream with comedian Shank Comics. The singer gave a playful new reason for always keeping one eye covere...

item.title: President Marcos faces declining approval amid legislative and foreign policy challenges
item.description: A Pulse Asia survey revealed that President Ferdinand Bongbong Marcos Jr.'s national approval and trust ratings fell by 7% points each, while disapproval and distrust rose by 5% and 7% respectively. H...
